I'll rely on this established structure.

The key physical insight for inhibition: many small-molecule/antibody inhibitors of Aβ42 selectively block **secondary nucleation** (they coat fibril surfaces), leaving primary nucleation largely intact. This is molecularly correct and produces the observed lag-time extension without changing plateau.
